name: IGF1R mutations were linked to exceptional longevity and reduced receptor signaling
summary: The centenarian IGF1R study supports human genetic plausibility for lower insulin/IGF signaling in exceptional longevity.
statement: Functionally significant IGF1R variants were reported in Ashkenazi Jewish centenarians, especially women, and transformed lymphocytes carrying the variants showed reduced IGF1R signaling.
population_or_model: Ashkenazi Jewish centenarians, offspring, and comparison participants
time_horizon: Cross-sectional genetic and cellular functional analysis
quantitative_note: The source reports higher serum IGF-I in affected female offspring, consistent with compensatory response to reduced receptor signaling.
caveats: This is genetic association plus cell-signaling evidence, not an intervention trial.
The association is population- and sex-specific and requires cautious generalization.
High serum IGF-I in carriers complicates simple low-IGF biomarker interpretation.
